content: EPISODE 2 FLIGHT OF THE CHIMAERA
ACT 1 IMPERIAL ACADEMY

During the next 6 weeks aboard the Chimaera, you begin a regimented schedule every morning, which repeats every single day without fail. Every morning, at exactly 0600 the ship lights flare on and Reveille is sounded. At approximately 0715 the first morning Captain Pelleon enters your quarters and personally hands you both sealed orders. The orders are signed by Grand Admiral Thrawn himself. They detail that you are required to complete a course called ‘Basic Imperial Officer Training, Accelerated.’ You have 6 weeks to pass the Final Exam, which will be given to you at the time. It is supposedly an oral exam, physical exam, and skill evaluation all in one.  Every morning you join a dozen other Cadets on the Flight Deck and run laps, do push ups, and march in formation, starting at 0700 sharp. At 0800 you start classes all the way to 0500, stopping between 1200 and 0100 for lunch break. Classes include Imperial Military Doctrine, Mathematics, Gun Range, Engineering, Computing, Philosophy, and Piloting with Simulators. In the afternoon you’re required to do dense technical reading, and pass tests at the end of every week, including performance exams in marksmanship and piloting. You occasionally have time to rest, most of your life becomes consumed with this Imperial Training School. Six arduous weeks pass. It is now finally, your last day. Today is what it’s all be building up for: the Final Exam. You find yourself in a huddle at 0655 as you have finished showering, stuffed down breakfast, and slapped on your uniform to report to the Flight Deck of the Chimaera for your in person live Final Examination.
You fellow Imperial students trickle in one by one, grumbling that whatever happens, it'll finally be over.

At 0659 the students all line up single file at attention along the Flight Deck. At 0700 on the nose, a procession of 4 Death Troopers escorts Grand Admiral Thrawn, Captain Pellaeon, and Rukh... followed up shortly by all 7 of the Imperial Academy's Instructors.

Lexi stands at attention with the other officers in training. She fixes her gaze on the wall ahead of her, not needing to see the procession to follow their progress. The last several weeks had been gruelling, and exhausting. Her work seemed almost double as she spent additional time catching up to grasp concepts the others took for granted. She's barely slept, having relied mostly on her connection on the Force to revitalize and sustain her. The night before was the fist she'd thrown her hands up and decided sleep was the best preparation she could do.

**"Good morning Cadets, as you already know, I am Grand Admiral Thrawn. What you may *not* be aware of is that, on the way to the top, I earned Flawless Marks at the Imperial Training Academy on Coruscant. Every Test you are about to undergo, know that I have passed each with a Perfect Score. While I would be delighted if any of you were to surpass my scores back in my heyday... I'll settle at 70% for a Passing Score here today. Since you have 7 Separate Tests, you must pass at least 5 in order to be Graduated."** There is a general nervous shifting and fidgeting in the line of Cadets at attention. Thrawn clicks his heels and salutes the line of Cadets in a practiced manner, crossing both arms in front of him in an 'X' shape, proclaiming loudly: **"For the Resurrection of The True Empire!"** The line of Cadets gives a practiced vigorous full-throated response at the tops of their lungs: **"True Empire!"** The young voices echo around the large chamber. Thrawn nods at the Cadets, **"Instructors, you may carry on about your business. I've matters to attend elsewhere, but I shall return to pin your Officer Tags on your chest at the end of the day. Good luck to you all. Troopers, aboutface on 2. 1 and 2."** The Death Troopers execute a 180 degree turn whilst covering the grand admiral and their clicking boots recede, giving room to the First Instructor of the Day, and the First Test.
Your First Test is an Oral Exam regarding Imperial Military Doctrine. This deals with rank plates, titles, and chain of command, as well as general Imperial War Tactics.
During this test, each Cadet in line must step forward and answer 4 out of 5 oral questions correctly.
Lexi waits for her turn, listening as the others ahead of her answer their required questions.

A stern-looking Officer approaches each in the long line of Cadets, until he arrives to stand before Lexi. He stands directly confronting you and clears his throat. **"Cadet Lexi Ardena. Your first question. An easy one, even for a little Jedi princess."** To your embarassment, half the class chuckles at that comment, bringing a tinge of red to your cheeks, and flustering you as you go to attempt an answer. **"How many Stormtroopers are there in a Standard Imperial Company?"**

Over the course of the six weeks she's had to patiently withstand the jibes of the instructors and other cadets a like. The redness on her cheeks from the sudden embarrassment was the only gratification she would give them as she takes a breath to calm the sudden pit in her stomach. "**Sir, there are 128 troopers in a standard company.**" She says with the correct intonations that had been drilled into them as the way to answer.

**"Correct, that's One Point, Cadet. Don't flatter yourself, at least 93% get that one right. Your next question won't be as easy."** He flicks his datapad and smiles a mirthless smile: **"Second Question. Name the Battle in which the whole Rebel Fleet and Luke Skywalker were defeated by The True Emperor, forever ending the Rebel Alliance."**
"**Battle of Endor,**" she replies, the events of which had been enscribed to her memory when she passed through the World Between Worlds.

**"Two Points. You're halfway there. Astonishing. Is there actually a brain inside there?"** He seems to cheer up ever so slightly, not overtly, but in the withdrawal of his cold impassionate tone. You get the sense he didn't expect you to pass at all, and now you've peaked his curiosity. The Instructor consults his Datapad, **"Oh I like this one - it has a trick answer. Third Question. How many Imperial Grand Admirals served the True Empire?"**
Lexi blinks at him. "**The position can be held by twelve at a time.**"

Lexi thinks back on her gruelling indoctrination sessions, night after night cramming for endless exams one after the next. The term True Empire, as she recalls it, refers to Thrawn's Organization. The History of this name is an interesting one. Few citizens of the Galaxy know the true inner workings of Palpatine's Court. However, Thrawn claims to have special knowledge and disputes the Official Narrative of the Empire. According to Thrawn's Propaganda, Emperor Palpatine the First was someone Thrawn calls The True Emperor. Thrawn claims that in a freak Sith experiment gone wrong, shortly after The Battle of Endor, the True Emperor was killed by a younger clone of himself. Donning the True Emperor's Persona, the Clone has steered the Galactic Empire away from prosperity into an age of tyrannical pan-Galactic totalitarianism. Thusly positioned, Thrawn seeks to draw individuals to serve him in the pursuit of the destruction of the Galactic Empire, in order to return to the greatness of the True Empire of old.
She skips a beat as she realizes the trick and before the instructor moves on she adds, "**However, there was a secret Grand Admiral under the True Emporer, bringing the number to thirteen.**"

**"Blast... you figured it out."** The smarmy officer had been ready to cut in with a harsh rebuke regarding your initial answer, but he had to let you finish in order to be fair, and to his utter disappointment you figured out the trick. He frowns deeply, realizing now just one answer away from a perfect score. **"Where do you Jedi get off trying to one-up my class at an Imperial Academy. I'm going to take the liberty of reaching for the Very High Difficulty Question Bank for this next one, just out of pure spite. Do you have anything to say in objection to my executive decision, Cadet Lexi Ardena?"** He angrily fidgets at his datapad, flicking frantically until his gaze settles on a particularly devious question, he looks up at you once more, ready to bust you back to Boot Camp.

Thrawn had warned her and Wraith the first day that their status as Force users would require them to prove themselves more than the others. Generally it was less blatant, but as the instructor goes to the bottom of his list in an attempt to internationally fail her, she can't restrain the frustrated anger that surgee through her.  At least for a moment before her Jedi training kicks in and she watches the officer with her usual neutral expression. Her lapse only noticeable by another attuned to her emotions.

**"Your Fourth Question, young lady. Yes... This is a real, actual question on the Final Exam for *Imperial* Officers. Question Four. Describe in detail how to construct a Standard Imperial Star Destroyer. "** Murmurs of shock ripple through the rest of the Cadets.

Lexi can't help but to grimace as the instructor asks his fourth question. As much as she had studied this was one facet she'd been weakest. Instead she tries to stall to give herself more time. "**Shall I presume one of the major ship yards are being utilized, sir. Kuat perhaps?**"

**"Yes yes let's assume a ship yard that has all the necessary power, men and material to construct ships en masse. Now then, Cadet, clock is ticking..."** He makes a gesture at his wrist, one finger tapping.

Lexi let's out a breath before drawing in a calming one. While she had tried to stall to wrack her memory for a suitable answer, one doesn't come to her.  Instead she organizer her thoughts on what she knows about the ship in regards from it's material size to the required crew compliment. The details on the construction of the capital ship completely beyond her knowledge.

Several beats pass, and the line of Cadets begins to murmur more loudly than before, harsh whispers interrupting by occasional gasps. The uncomfortable scene plays out as the Imperial Instructor continues to taunt Lexi. After several more minutes of gloating, someone finally pipes up. **"If you'll pardon my interruption sir! Such a question is utterly ridiculous!"** Wraith steps forward from the straight line of cadets, still facing dead ahead.
Head whirls around, **"Who said that!?"**

Lexi breaks her focus on the wall ahead of her to follow the officers gaze to Wraith. It was bad enough the instructor had decided to single her out, but now Wraith has decided to interject and become irreparably involved.

The Instructor had whipped around to the source of the interruption, only to be halted by the terrifying Visage of Wraith, transformed and once more in possession of the Dark Side, igniting his lightsaber with a SHICK-HISS-THRUMMMM.

**"You know something, Instructor Vandrik Musenn? I never liked you very much, come to think of it. Now I remembered why. You enjoy picking on the weak and innocent, don't you? Does it make you feel Powerful, to lord your petty rank above those lower down in the Military Hierarchy!?"** Wraith had steadily advanced with purposeful strides directly at the Imperial Instructor, who himself had been pinwheeling and backing away, hand searching for the clasp to his blaster pistol holster.
**"You can't speak to me that way!! I'll have you disciplined and dumped in isolation for the rest of your miserable life if I so deem it~"**
Finally after fumbling the Instructor had unholstered his mostly decorative, but functional antique blaster pistol - holding it directly at Wraith's head.

**"What's more, come to think of it, today is the last day of our training isn't it? That means your job is over, and you're no longer useful to me, doesn't it? That effectively means your continued existence is no longer required."**

The waves of Dark Side energy from Wraith wash over her and her eyes widen. She hadn't seen him like this since the first day when he had chased her through the portal. All the work they had done to get the others to trust them was about to be undone. 
Seeing the officer draw his weapon finally and aim it Wraith she decides to take action. She puts herself between Wraith and the officer, hoping to stop Wraith from escalating the situation further.

"**Stop,**" she says softly to Wraith, her tone no less commanding in it's intensity. She turns her back to the officer as she faces what she considers the more pressing danger. Her hand goes unconsciously to her lightsaber, though she doesn't draw it. "**You're letting your anger get the better of you. Think about what is happening here.**"

Your impassioned words seem to hit their mark, as Wraith visibly gets rocked by your statement. His confident expression falters for the first time, and his poised lightsaber hovers a little lower. However, the saber suddenly snaps up in an even more aggressive pose, as Wraith's face suddenly becomes even more enraged. **"What's happening here is this Imperial wants to make an example out of you, and you're defending him from a well deserved death. Move away, I want to kill him so badly I won't hesitate to go through anyone. Not even you."** His yellow Dark-Side corrupted eyes turn to regard Lexi for the first time, and you can see an endless soulless abyss of madness deep in those glowing yellow orbs.

Lexi doesn't budge as his attention comes to her. Ignoring his threat, she continues in her soft tones, "**We knew it was inevitably going to happen.**" She pauses making sure his attention remained on her. "**It's been no secret to what I am and I won't be ashamed of it.  There will be those too ignorant to look past lies and prejudices.**" Eyeing his lit saber, she gestures towards it. "**The end of a lightsaber is no way to teach the ignorant.**"

By this time, about a dozen Stormtroopers had taken up firing positions on the trio occupying the center of everyone's attention. A second Imperial Instructor had been summoned and was holding his hand up signifying for the Stormtroopers to hold their fire.

The Imperial had been steadily becoming more unhinged, his pistol wavering in his hand. Finally, without warning a loud BANG is heard by Lexi's left ear, not enough to blow out that eardrum, but giving it a good run for its credits. On automatic reflex, Wraith's lightsaber deflects the blaster bolt back at the Imperial Instructor. It catches the man in the shoulder and spins him around to the ground, knocking him out as he collapses. Everyone present clearly saw what just happened.

After a long beat, the new Instructor makes a rodeo gesture with his right hand, and the dozen Stormtroopers descend on Lexi and Wraith, shoving blaster carbines into both your faces. Advancing not far behind the Stormtroopers, the new Imperial Officer walks up to the duo, after the lightsaber had been secured by his guards. **"Well well well, that was a highly foolish action, wasn't it. NK-521 and NK-601 if you would be so kind... Escort Instructor Musenn to the Med Bay immediately."**
Two Stormtroopers detach and drag Musenn out of the Bay, grasping him under both armpits, feet dragging behind.

Lexi reflexively moves away from the loud bang as it blasts past her ear and she sees the result of the discharge as Wraith expertly sends it back towards the instructor. Her ear is ringing as she finds herself surrounded at gunpoint. Tentatively she holds out her hands as she watches Wraith carefully, concerned he would continue on his previous course.
Wraith had been shocked at the man's demise and had permitted himself to be disarmed by gunpoint by no less than 7 different Stormtroopers.

**"Now then, everyone remain calm and I'm going to officially hold everyone here under my direct command, until I'm satisfied with my investigation."** By this time, the crowd of Cadets had formed a circle around the action, and were beginning to grumble once more.
**"Does anyone present wish to give a report on what just happened?"**
Her hearing still off on the one side she turns her head so she can better hear the weapon officer's words. "**I would, sir,**" she volunteers, slowly her voice sounding funny to her.
**"Alright, and you are?"**
His face lands on Lexi closely for the first time, and you see in his eyes recognition, he remembers you from his classes in melee combat.
And especially from his classes at the gun range.

"**Cadet Lexi Ardena,**" she says by way of introduction as was expected of her here as part of the training courses. "**Instructor Musenn was proctering my questions for the exam, sir. His fourth question was pulled from the High Difficulty set of questions. Cadet Kai felt the question unfair and took it upon himself to confront Instructor Musenn.**"

Holds an outstretched hand, palm facing you: **"I've just picked up Musenn's Datapad, and I see you're indeed correct. This question is far outside the difficulty setting for novice Imperial Officers. I believe this constitutes enough evidence to verify what you claim. Now onto the next question, who authorized Cadet Kai to active his personalized Special Operation Gear in the middle of the Final Exam at my Academy?"** He rounds on Wraith, **"Have you anything to say in your defense, Cadet Kai?"**

Wraith had recovered his composure, thanks to Lexi having taken the spotlight off him for a brief moment, although his eyes were still yellow they appeared more as simmering embers, his thirst for violence having been temporarily slaked. **"Only this, sir. My saber is classified as SpecOps Gear sure, but it is also my Primary Personal Defense Weapon. Today it was used in the latter capacity only, sir, not in its capacity as a Special Operations Tool. Sir."**

**"Well phrased, Cadet. If you hadn't pulled that little excuse I'd be cuffing you right now. But you're technically correct. Technically correct: the best type of correct."** He walks over to retrieve Wraith's lightsaber from one of the Stormtroopers and return it to Wraith's waiting hand. **"Your Primary Weapon has been returned to you. And here I was on my way to administer the Second Exam, thought I'd show up a few minutes sooner, but here you two go and make Musenn finally shut his pie hole just in time for me to take my turn at administering your Finals. You're all lucky you saved me time. I don't want to sit and wait all day while Instructor Musenn makes you Cadets recite a construction recipe for a whole damn star cruiser."** He waves away the Stormtroopers. **"Back in line, Cadets, all of you. Judging by your still being here, you've all passed the First Exam. It is time for your Second Exam to begin."**

During the Second Exam, each candidate underwent a test of either Marksmanship or Melee Combat skills. This test came in two varieties. Every Cadet had the choice of either ranged or melee weapon. In the case of a Ranged Weapon, the candidate had to successfully hit a target at 100 meters, 300 meters, and 500 meters with a minimum accuracy of 70%. In the case of a Melee weapon, each candidate had to hold their own without being knocked down, versus Instructor Roat in nonlethal melee, for 100 seconds. The latter had proved too hard for most, so most Cadets opted for testing Marksmanship. 

The Third, Fourth, Fifth and Sixth Exam was administered by Datapad, constituting the written portion of the Finals. These questions covered many topics including Engineering, Computing, Philosophy, Mathematics, and Science.

The Seventh and Final Exam is finally here. Lexi had to pass this last one in order to graduate from the Academy, having flunked two of the written sections due to the frenzied pace of her studies. Now it would all come down to the Flight Simulators, which were the sole focus of the last and Final Test. To absolutely no one’s surprise, Grand Admiral Thrawn had rejoined the group of aspiring Cadets once more, but this time he was far above every candidate, inside the Control Tower of the Hangar Bay. Therein, large monitors were stationed that would allow the Chiss to be able to see the Simulated Battle unfold and enable him to better judge this crop of Officer candidates.

He announced to the entire Hangar Bay: **“Cadets, this is Grand Admiral Thrawn speaking. Your final test has arrived. Thus far, those of you still standing at attention have passed. However, now comes your True Test, at last.”** Hitting a button on the control board, a large holoprojection displayed in the Hangar Bay, showing ships arrayed for battle on two sides. **“For your final test, I’m sending you into a simulated space battle, one from earlier on in my career. Each Cadet must prove their worth in their skills as a pilot, and survive against a full flight of opposing squadrons, outnumbered 4 to 1. Of course you have the advantage of piloting the Empire’s most advanced TIE variant to date, but such is your right as true Imperial Officers. Those of you shot down will have failed, at the battle’s conclusion only those remaining in their cockpits, with their vital signs intact, shall earn the right to wear the Badge of Imperial Navy Lieutenant.”** Coordinated with this speech, your instructors, up until that point on standby, unveil a board studded with blue badges, each badge having four blue squares in a single row.

The tests have been rigorous and exhausting to this point, but the end point was approaching. Lexi eyes the board after it's unveiled, the badges prominently displayed on it. A part of her knew the flight simulator would be the deciding factor to whether she'd be able to move forward with the arrangement she and Wraith had made with Thrawn. For the moment she doesn't consider what her next steps would have to be should she not pass.
The majority of the Cadets couldn't help but stare, their goal literally within reach. It was fitting of Imperial Cruelty to dangle something so tantalizing within reach.
**"Cadets, to your Flight Simulators!"**
Entering the Flight Sim, the whole outer world shuts down. Inside, flickering lights gradually signal the startup of a TIE Defender, all systems reading as green.

Lexi heads to her simulator, catching Wraith's gaze for a moment and gives him a small smile. Disappearing into the cramped cockpit of the simulated TIE, she starts going through the motions of getting situated and checks the console readouts.
With everything reading ready to go, she waits for the directed all clear to launch and the targeting orders.

As soon as the Shared Countdown reaches Zero, all the TIE Defenders are released from the simulated Hangar Bay, into outer space. A single giant rocky irregularly shaped enemy Capital Ship looms ahead, spitting out Alien Starfighters. Before you can even get your bearings, it seems your group of Defenders is swarmed.

Her head swivels one way and the another as she takes in the confusing readouts from the sensors, but she quickly gives in the futility and instead relies on her other senses and reflexes to get her through the swarm. She joins her wing mate and tries to figure out where the rest of the squadron is.

It seems that you're up against a particularly determined group of Alien Pirates, who have salvaged parts of ships from X-Wings, Z-95 Headhunters, as well as new starfighter components, to create ships with capabilities rivaling that of Imperial Engineers.

As you're about to be gunned down by a determined opponent, Wraith's TIE Defender comes in to blast the enemy into a gas cloud at the last moment. His DIE defender pulls up alongside. **"There you are. Let's see if we can't even the odds up a bit."**

Lexi, in coordination with Wraith, you instinctively know you'll be able to target down your opposition, while maintaining a high enough speed to discourage any more pursuit with your wing mate alongside, although you find yourself facing a difficult choice. Your less experienced fellow Cadets are getting slaughtered, due to their slow reaction times. To attempt to pierce the Enemy Formation and break the main enemy swarm would be approaching suicide, but to leave the Cadets to die could also be considered cowardly. Your first choice presents itself clearly: should you risk everything in an attempt to mount a heroic attack or should you stay with Wraith picking off opponents methodically, to eventually win a war of attrition, while sacrificing your fellow students in the process as bait.

Lexi can't help but grin as her opponent is taken out by Wraith. "**Sounds good to me,**" she says lightly over the comms.  As they begin to pick off targets she notices the situation other less experienced cadets had found themselves in. 
To her it wasn't much of a choice, and if she got Wraith to follow her then the chances of success would increase significantly. "**Follow my lead. We're going to break through to the others.**" All she can do now is hope he follows her as she begins the assault to break through the formation.

His voice comes back over the comms with an edge of amusement, **"Great. Somehow, I knew you'd say that... Resetting shields forward 90%, suggest you do the same, switching to aft after we pass them. Let's gun it. If we come in fast and hard, we might be able to draw away enough attention and give time for the other students to organize into pairs and begin to mount a meaningful counterattack."** His TIE Defender's drive flare white-blue, after yours. **"Lead on."**
Over comms, one of the Cadets yells out, **"I can't shake him, someone get him off me--."** She's cut off into static.

Now set to their course, Lexi does as he suggests and resets the shields, ready to flip them once they make it past the line. She aims for the largest concentration of the cadets with the intent of getting enough pressure off of them to join in a more meaningful attack formation and move onto others. If she has the opportunity to help individual pilots shake a fighter, much like Wraith had done for her earlier she does so.

Acceleration in space is theoretically infinite, to built up enough speed both you and Wraith swing wide in an arc, using the time away from the densest clump of fighters to build up your momentum. Finally you both rocket through to pierce the enemy formation at its heart. The speeds are so great that you first begin to feel dizzy, but as you approach the heart of the fight, a combination of the Force and simple animal adrenaline slows down the action marginally to allow you to steer and guide your TIE Defender true. Both Lexi and Wraith synchronize and fire a Volley of Concussion Missiles as you pass the Heart of the Enemy Formation, both TIE Defenders blowing past, followed in your wake by a series of brilliant Concussion Missile Explosions as 8 enemy ships are targeted and turned to scrap. Hoots and shouts of encouragement are heard over comms, as now the easy part is done, and you find an angry swarm of Pirates on your tail.
**"Well, the good news is, we've got their attention."**

"**Let's see if we can keep it long enough for the others to get their act together.**" The adrenaline courses through her as she checks the status of the aft shields after resetting them. "**I'll keep their attention. Let's see if you can't pick them off.**" With the swarm on their heels, she intends to give them a merry chase hoping to set up shots for Wraith and any of the other cadets they come across to pick them off.

At first, the Pirates are a bit confused, but by instinct as soon as you split up with Wraith, half the pirates continue to chase each of you, plinking away precious shields off your rear deflectors. After a tense chase, your shields go down completely, signified by a flickering lights inside your cockpit. You instinctively hold your breath knowing that any second could be your last, inside the simulation. At the very last second, a group of TIE Defenders intercept the pirates chasing you down and begin a whole new engagement. After another minute, the enemy Pirates seem to have been routed. Cheers and clapping is heard over comms, as the lights dim completely and the hatch to the Simulator opens, letting in the illumination of the Hangar Bay once more. Clapping and whistles can still be heard, but this time in real life.
Some of the Cadet come up to Lexi to shake your hand or pat you roughly on the back and shoulder. **"Cadets! Atten-- Hutt!"** The students line up once more.
**Admiral on Deck!"**

Lexi let's her breath out in a rush as the simulation ends. She takes a deep breath to quell the adrenaline pumping through her body from the fight.  Exiting the simulator she's surprised as some of the cadets come up to her. 
With the call to attention she falls into line with the others, sparing a moment to wipe the sweat from her forehead before it has a chance to get in her eyes.

Thrawn strides up to the line of Cadets, hands folded behind his back, followed by the rolling board full of Naval Lieutenant Badges, pushed by his Death Troopers, who each deliberately and roughly pin the badges to the naval cadets pectorals: **"Cadets, today you demonstrated exceptional courage and ingenuity. Each of you survivors has earned this commendation through your own blood sweat and tears. However..."** He stops as he reaches Lexi and Wraith, who stand shoulder to shoulder, **"I rather think the biggest surprise of the day was the outstanding performance of Lieutenant Kai and Lieutenant Ardena. I personally oversaw everything you two accomplished: organizing a daring attack, risking your own careers, in hopes of rallying the rest of the troops to victory. Very impressive, and worthy of being singled out for praise."**  His eyes sparkle, smiling evily, **"We will watch your careers with great interest."** Finishing off the rest of the ceremony, Thrawn concludes with the, by now, routine battlecry for the True Empire as usual, before leaving the Bay. The Instructors shake the hands of some of the Cadets and everyone has now shifted to celebration mode, Wraith gliding up to you as you're finally able to speak freely.

**"Saving the whole class... All in a day's work for the strikingly beautiful Jedi Princess. I saw you eating up the attention back there, just don't forget your humble wing mate when you become the talk of the mess hall."**

She laughs, his comment of praise not escaping her. "**How could I forget. I doubt it would have worked had we not done it together.**" Her elation at the whole ordeal being over gives her a lightness to her step. "**It's finally over. I haven't felt so relieved since the completion of my Trials.**"

Thanks to Wraith hovering nearby, none of the students bother you two, remembering the Reaper going psycho all of the first Instructor. You make your way to the edge of the Hangar Bay, overlooking the endless field of Stars beyond the containment field. Staring off into the distance, we zoom out on our two heroes aboard the Chimaera.

Lexi looks out at the endless field of stars, her thoughts starting to wander. She’s glad the other cadets have decided to leave the two of them alone for the moment. For all his teasing, she isn’t used to being singled out for praise. Glancing around she notices the other newly minted officers beginning to form groups and leave, some of them casting glances their way. Their fear from Wraith’s most recent show of force is obvious and accounts for why she and Wraith are being given a wide berth..
Turning back as if to look out into the void, she looks at Wraith peripherally. “**What happened earlier?**” she asks slowly, fully expecting him to deflect the question.

Wraith flings around, eyes sharply locking with her's. **"Nothing that will be spoken of."** Biting his lip, he regrets the harshness in his tone. Backing off a bit, he answered in a more calm, mannered matter. **"Let's say that being raised by hyponogogic indoctrination didn't exactly do wonders for my psychological wellbeing. Many a clone in my position struggle with Clone Psychosis. Your question is not unexpected, but unfortunately there is nothing you can do to help by continuing to bring attention to the problem."** Smiling, Wraiths lightens his tone and adds: **"Just try not to get on my bad side."**

She nods slowly, accepting his answer as something of an accomplishment.  As he tries to lighten the tone of the conversation, she gives him a small smile of her own. She's nonplussed by his implied threat, which even with his light tone she knew it for what it was. "**I'll keep that in mind. As long as you remember: the last thing I want is a repeat because of me.**" She glances about the bay once more.
Deciding to change the topic of their conversation, she says, "**It looks like the others have gone off to celebrate. Likely to the mess. Shall we join them?**"
**"You go on, I'm not exactly the... socializing type."** He straightens, making ready to head off, **"I've some business to take care of, myself."**
"**If you're sure,**" she looks up at him for a moment. "**I'll see you later, then.**" With a small wave in farewell she heads out herself and makes her way to the mess.
Youre aboard the Chimaera. You may visit the Command Bridge (C1), Mess Hall (C2), Personal Quarters (C0), Landing Bay (C3), and many other areas.

Strolling briskly through cold corridors, you begin to quickly get lost around the nameless passageways. It's like a termite mound, is the closest you can relate this to. Was it wise to set off without a guide?
Suddenly, you bump into a stranger at an intersection where neither could see the other until after the collision.

Lexi sighs, frustrated that even after six weeks, she still manages to get herself lost on the vast ship. Her thoughts distracted, she collides with another as they both reach the intersection at the same time. "**Oh, I'm sorry!**" She looks over the other person with genuine concern.

Neither have slept for at least 24 hours, both Wraith's and Lexi's body have been abused, during your wait for Admiral Thrawn you had both gotten patched up and bandaged by a Medic.

Lexi's mind is a whirl as she hears him stalk off into the common refresher. She had turned her back to him so he wouldn't see her expression; she doesn't have a mask to hide behind like he does. Not moving from her spot, she listens to the destruction he causes from his anger.

Unable to look into her eyes any longer, he seems to become hesitant of himself. After all, Wraith had never experienced a willing partner before. Lexi face goes scarlet at the suddenness of his impropriety, but she can't resist watching him as he strides into the fresher. I'll only end up doing this again to you, over and over again, until there is nothing left. The Dark Side is a Pure Force of Chaos and Destruction."** He looks into her eyes, seeing the desire for human affection and connection, something he could never grant her even if he wanted. His eyes start to glow cyan, as he again attunes his mind to her own through the Force, **"There is only One Way to Exorcise Evil... You Know It Full Well... Free the Galaxy from the Iron Fist of the Dark Side, Chosen One. That is the Only Way to not only Free Me, but... to Free Them All."**

We see views of various rooms aboard the Chimaera. Thrawn sitting in his Command Throne, hands steepled. Young Cadets laughing in a small group, one of them maimed and missing a limb. Gears turning as a TIE Defender ratchets along its railway gantry, slowly being guided by a hangar bay technician.
Wraith's eyes radiate such fire that they begin to burn Lexi's retinas. **"One Day... Very Soon... Thanks to you... Our Dark Chains, Shall Be Broken."**

Lexi meets his gaze and sees his despair from their failed attempts to be close to each other. His Darker nature would always come between them, threatening to destroy her. The memory of it nearly sends her into another fit. He didn’t seem to remember what it was he had done to her, but his own experience had filled in the blanks from the state he had found her in. Once he had warned her she would not want to find herself a Reaper’s prisoner. After her brief experience, his suggestion to have a means to end her life before then seemed more prudent.

Her morbid thoughts shift when Lexi feels the change in him as he draws on their Bond. Once more he calls her out as the Chosen One. Like his Dark Side state, she wonders if he retains this knowledge. The Prophecy of the Chosen One hangs heavy on her and she wonders if Anakin Skywalker ever felt the weight of this burden. Had he been aware of the hopes and expectations being placed on him? 

It is hard to feel anything but inadequate to the task when she feels nothing but weariness from her battered body. The last thing she recalls as her body succumbs to exhaustion is Wraith’s burning gaze and his Prophecy.
